_comment: All tunable thresholds in ONE place. LOCKED operator decisions are marked. The sentinel reads these; it never rewrites them (a threshold change rides the repo + rollout, like all EWS data).

tick._locked: D3: every 15 minutes per box. Zero model calls; cadence costs CPU only.

aggregator._locked: D3/D4: operator-box aggregator hourly; a box with no fresh tick for 2 consecutive cycles = P1 'sentinel dark'.

context._locked: D5: running-low (>=70 note, >=85 handoff) is a LOCAL matter routed to the BOX'S OWN agent so it self-handles (handoff/memory flush). The OPERATOR is alerted ONLY for the broken-config case (effective ceiling <= 0, or softThresholdTokens >= contextWindow) - a crash-no-matter-what only the operator can repair. NARROW EXCEPTION (approved, scoped to the OPERATOR'S OWN BOX ONLY - Trevor's box, never a client box): when `operator_self_notify` is true (default), an S3|handoff finding ALSO sends ONE deduped, plain-language self-notice to the operator's own chat ('your assistant's working memory is N% full...'), because on that one box the finding's SUBJECT and the operator are the same person - it is the owner's own session health, not an ops alert about someone else's box. This is gated STRUCTURALLY in code on the box's own ledger role meta being 'operator' (ews_alert.py route_finding, Lane 2), never on this flag alone; flipping this flag can only turn the notice OFF on the operator box, it can never turn it ON for a client box. Everything else in this skill stays client-silent, unchanged.

escalation._locked: D4: an unacknowledged P1 goes to the Rescue Rangers channel after 30 minutes; a dead-man P1 escalates immediately.

snapshots._locked: D7: keep the LARGER of 60 snapshots or 45 days. Pruning is a P3 digest line.

furnace._locked: D9: HYBRID. On-box detection first; escalate to a READ-ONLY provider usage/balance check only when on-box flags something. Billing-aware framing per config/billing-models.json.

caps._locked: D2: ALERT-ONLY fleet-wide. enforce_caps defaults OFF on every box; it is a documented per-box opt-in only, never a fleet default.

self_update._locked: D8: WEEKLY, PINNED. Starts weekly and STAYS weekly; it must NOT auto-downshift. The recommender may surface a note, but the default cadence never changes itself.
